title: guarea macrophylla subsp. pachycarpa
intro: Guarea macrophylla subsp. pachycarpa, also known as Guarea pachycarpa, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains various compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Guarea pachycarpa include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the bark is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, while the leaves can be used to make a tea or applied topically as a poultice.
